items.question: GNB predicts the class with the highest:
options: Sum of squared errors to class centroids
Log-posterior score: log P(y=k) + Σ_j log N(x_j; μ_{k,j}, σ²_{k,j})
items.answer: Log-posterior score: log P(y=k) + Σ_j log N(x_j; μ_{k,j}, σ²_{k,j})
items.explanation: Gaussian NB combines class prior with summed log Gaussian class-conditional likelihoods under the naive factorization.

items.question: Training GaussianNB primarily involves:
options: Gradient descent on cross-entropy for many epochs
Estimating class priors and per-class per-feature means and variances
Building a k-d tree over training points
Learning support vectors and margin weights
items.answer: Estimating class priors and per-class per-feature means and variances
items.explanation: GNB fit is closed-form MLE of Gaussian parameters — no iterative optimizer.

items.question: Gaussian Naive Bayes is best described as:
options: A discriminative linear classifier in log-odds space
A generative classifier using Normal P(x_j|y) per feature
items.answer: A generative classifier using Normal P(x_j|y) per feature
items.explanation: GNB models P(x|y) with Gaussian likelihoods then applies Bayes — generative, not direct P(y|x) like logistic regression.

items.question: In sklearn GaussianNB, theta_ stores:
items.explanation: theta_ is the fitted mean matrix with shape (n_classes, n_features) after fit.

items.question: Toy setup: P(A)=0.6, μ_A=4, σ²_A=1; P(B)=0.4, μ_B=7, σ²_B=2; x=5. Which class wins?
items.explanation: x=5 is closer to A's mean; log(0.6)+log N(5|4,1) ≈ −1.93 beats log(0.4)+log N(5|7,2) ≈ −3.33.

items.question: The naive independence assumption in GNB means:
options: P(x|y) ≈ ∏_j P(x_j|y) — features independent given class y
items.answer: P(x|y) ≈ ∏_j P(x_j|y) — features independent given class y
items.explanation: Conditional independence given y lets GNB multiply per-feature Gaussian likelihoods — full treatment in Conditional Independence Assumption.
